title: OpenClaw Tool Reference
description: Each tools/<tool-id>.json file in an agent workspace conforms to this schema. See spec/openclaw-v1/003-tool-contract.md.

id.description: Kebab-case unique id within this agent.

description.description: One-line summary, used in the architect's tool listings.

tool_kind.description: Either a built-in kind (workspace-read, workspace-write, sandbox-exec, research, plan-validate) or a custom kebab-case id registered with the runtime.

config.description: Tool-specific configuration. Validated against the tool's own schema by the runtime.

stages.description: Lifecycle stages where this tool may be invoked. Values match the AgentDevStage enum from spec section 002 (lifecycle states). Omit or null = all stages. The earlier draft mixed copilot phase names (plan/build/review/test/ship/reflect) with lifecycle stages — those have been removed; copilot phases are not stages.

modes.description: Execution modes where this tool may be invoked. Omit or null = all modes.

read_only.description: True if this tool only reads state and produces no side effects on workspace, memory, config, network egress, subprocess, or external services. Maps to the runtime's isReadOnly() method. Auto-allowed in build mode without human approval. Tools that lie about this fail the conformance fuzzer (see 101).

destructive.description: True if this tool can cause irreversible changes (delete files, send messages, deploy). Maps to the runtime's isDestructive() method. Always requires human approval unless availableModes excludes destructive contexts. Mutually exclusive with read_only=true.

concurrency_safe.description: True if multiple invocations may run in parallel without observable interference. Maps to isConcurrencySafe(). Tools with shared mutable state are not concurrency-safe.

requires_approval.description: If true, every invocation pauses for human approval regardless of mode. Defaults to whatever the destructive flag implies, unless explicitly set.

allOf.description: A read-only tool cannot also be destructive.

ref.description: Key into the credential store. The runtime resolves this to actual secrets at execution time.

schema_ref.description: Path to JSON Schema describing the resolved credential shape.
